expectations.evidence: plays `c` three times, but compressed into the same time window as a single `c`. The notes play faster — it's like looping at higher speed within the same duration slot.

expectations.text: Explains the ! operator (replicate at normal speed)
expectations.evidence: shorthand for `c c c` — three separate events each at normal speed. You're adding copies, not speeding up.

expectations.text: Makes clear that * is faster while ! keeps same tempo
expectations.evidence: `*` makes events faster (same duration slot, more events packed in) ... `!` adds more events at the same speed (each copy has full duration)

expectations.text: Includes code examples showing both * and ! usage
expectations.evidence: note("c*3")  // c repeated 3x fast — fits in 1 c-slot ... note("c!3")  // c c c — three normal-speed events
